content.claim: More than 60% of participating Hungarian schools reported needing additional special education teachers, psychologists, or pedagogical assistants; the number of such specialist staff doubled from 2010 to approximately 2023, but geographic distribution remains uneven, with one county reporting an average of 39.6 students per special education teacher.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Absolute workforce numbers and regional breakdown are incomplete
The adequacy benchmark (students per specialist) is reported for only one county, limiting generalizability
Data does not distinguish demand growth attributable to increased identification versus other factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
